design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot customizable HERC units through branching missions, salvage enemy equipment and weapons, upgrade your squad's Hercs for increasingly difficult encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (salvage accumulation) vs. mission objectives; tactical squad composition vs. enemy encounters; choosing between immediate mission success and equipment preservation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command advanced bipedal war machines (HERCs) as a military commander, making strategic choices about loadouts and squad composition while executing tactical combat operations across multiple Earth and space locations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Embraces sandbox-style mission failure with branching consequences rather than binary success/failure; prioritizes player agency in equipment assembly and squad customization over scripted progression; rewards strategic looting and resource management alongside combat skill

difficulty.continues_system: Mission-based checkpoint system with branching continuations; failure does not always end campaign, often routes player to alternative missions based on success degree
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-level checkpoints; some missions are terminal if failed (final sequence leading to Prometheus on Luna), but most failures allow campaign continuation on alternate mission paths
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through Herc customization (chassis selection, hardpoint equipment mounting), squad composition (up to 3 squadmates per mission), and equipment salvage decisions. Mission objectives have variable success thresholds (partial success possible), allowing players to calibrate challenge through resource commitment

progression.upgrade_system: Herc customization system: eight chassis types with varying armor, shield output, speed, and hardpoint counts. Equipment and weapons mount to chassis-specific hardpoints. Squadmates gain experience and improve across missions
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission rewards unlock new weapon types, equipment, or Herc chassis. Some equipment permanently unavailable if missions are failed; others become available later if missed initially. Terminal mission failures can lock players out of specific Herc types
replayability_hooks: Branching mission structure creates multiple campaign paths based on success/failure outcomes
Herc and equipment unlocks tied to specific missions; players must choose which missions to prioritize
Salvage-based loadout system encourages experimentation with different chassis and equipment combinations
Squad member experience progression incentivizes replaying missions to level underutilized squadmates

world_building_techniques: Pre-mission video briefings establish context and objectives
Post-mission debriefings with squadmate voice acting reinforce atmosphere and outcomes
Campaign setting across multiple continents and moon introduces scope (set in 2624, continuation of 20-year human-Cybrid conflict)
In-game intro cinematic establishes plot (Cybrid return from space colonies after initial defeat)

boss_design.philosophy: Terminal encounter serves as campaign climax rather than frequent mid-game gates; Prometheus on Luna is positioned as final confrontation that determines game ending
notable_bosses: Prometheus (Luna) - final campaign boss, encounter mandatory to complete game

faction_indicators: Human forces vs. Cybrid oppressors (primary conflict)
Squad cohesion through assigned and outfitted squadmates
Equipment choice signals military doctrine and playstyle allegiance
danger_signals: Destroyed Hercs and buildings indicating hostile territory presence
Mission branching structure creates high-stakes decision points where failure routes to different war theaters
Terminal missions (final Prometheus sequence) signal point of no return
environmental_storytelling: Salvageable wreckage from destroyed Hercs and buildings indicates previous combat and resource scarcity
Multi-continent and lunar campaign scope visually communicates scale of Cybrid threat
Terrain variety (rolling 3D terrain, multiple biomes across North/South America, Asia, Australia, Europe, Antarctica, Moon) reinforces global conflict setting
